ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH OR MODIFY SUBPOENA FOR DEPOSITION AND PRODUCTION OF RECORDS, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, A PROTECTIVE ORDER PROHIBITING THE OBTAINING OF DEPOSITION AND DOCUMENTS THROUGH SUBPOENA

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JAYANT AGARWAL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH, FIFTH, SIXTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANTS IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING THE MEANING OF THE 2009 BONUS TERMS. THE TERMS OF THE CONTRACT ARE AMBIGUOUS AND SUBJECT TO CONFLICTING PAROLE EVIDENCE (PLAINTIFF'S SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION PARAGRAPH 4 AND MENASHE'S DECLARATION PARAGRAPH 4). SEE WOLF V. SUPERIOR COURT (2004) 114 CAL APP 4TH 1343, 1350-1351. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
ORDER RE EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: CONTEMPT AND MOTION FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS

LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING HELD ON ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: CONTEMPT AND MOTION FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. PLAINTIFF IS FOUND IN CONTEMPT WITH REGARDS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION #38 AS FOLLOWS: 1- THE COURT FINDS BEYOND A REASONABLE DOUBT THAT THERE WAS A RENDITION OF A VALID ORDER DATED 8/13/10, SIGNED BY COMMISSIONER HEWLETT. 2- THE COURT FINDS BEYOND A REASONABLE DOUBT THAT PLAINTIFF HAD KNOWLEDGE OF THE ORDER. 3- THE COURT FINDS BEYOND A REASONABLE DOUBT THAT PLAINTIFF HAD THE ABILITY TO COMPLY WITH THE ORDER. 4- THE COURT FINDS BEYOND A REASONABLE DOUBT THAT PLAINTIFF WAS IN WILLFUL DISOBEDIENCE OF THE ORDER. AS TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 1, 2, AND 43, THE COURT DOES NOT FIND BEYOND A REASONABLE DOUBT THAT PLAINTIFF VIOLATED THE ORDER. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO PAY $1,000 IN SANCTIONS AND ATTORNEY FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,000 TO DEFENDANT'S ATTORNEY WITHIN 30 DAYS OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR #12353

Hearing On Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt And Motion For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-29-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Natalie Pierce, Esq., 415-288-6321, for Defendant/Cross-Complainat and moving party: Demeter Group Holdings, LP
Brenda D. Posada, Esq., Bradshaw & Associates, P.C., 415-433-4800, for Plaintiff and opposing party: Jayant Agarwal

Hearing held on Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt And Motion For Monetary Sanctions.
Plaintiff is found in contempt with regards to request for production #38 as follows:
1- The Court finds beyond a reasonable doubt that there was a rendition of a valid order dated 8/13/10, signed by Commissioner Hewlett.
2- The Court finds beyond a reasonable doubt that Plaintiff had knowledge of the order
3- The Court finds beyond a reasonable doubt that Plaintiff had the ability to comply with the order.
4- The Court finds beyond a reasonable doubt that Plaintiff was in willful disobedience of the order.
As to requests for production Nos. 1, 2, and 43, The Court does not find beyond a reasonable doubt that Plaintiff violated the Order.
Plaintiff is ordered to pay $1,000 in sanctions and Attorney fees in the amount of $2,000 to defendant's attorney within 30 days of this order. Order signed.
Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR #12353

MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-17-2010 Continued To Feb-09-11 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JAYANT AGARWAL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH, FIFTH, SIXTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANTS. Appearances: Natalie A. Pierce of Littler Mendelson, PC (288.6321) for defendant and responding party; Brenda D. Posada of Bradshaw & Associates, PC (433.4800) for plaintiff and moving party. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. There is a triable issue of fact regarding the meaning of the 2009 Bonus terms. The terms of the contract are ambiguous and subject to conflicting parole evidence (plaintiff's supplemental declaration paragraph 4 and Menashe's declaration paragraph 4). See Wolf v. Superior Court (2004) 114 Cal App 4th 1343, 1350-1351. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-10-2010 Set For Trial On Mar-21-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Mar-04-11 At 11:30 A.M. In Dept 303

Notice Of Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Objections Contained In Discovery Responses
Off Calendar Aug-17-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF JAYANT AGARWAL's Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Objections Contained In Discovery Responses is moot as the court made findings and orders as to the objections on August 5, 2010. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

Notice And Motion To Compel Further Responses, The Production Of Documents And Things, And The Frensic Examination Of Electronic Media
Off Calendar Aug-05-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Natalie A. Pierce, Esq., for defendant and moving party: DEMETER GROUP HOLDINGS, LP
Brenda D. Posada, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Jayant Agarwal

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT DEMETER GROUP HOLDINGS, LP's Motion To Compel Further Responses, The Production Of Documents And Things, And The Forensic Examination Of Electronic Media is granted in part and denied in part. The court finds that claims of privilege are not waived without a knowing waiver. The court further finds that the failure to timely respond was excusable neglect.
The motion is granted as to Nos. 1 and 2 as counsel are ordered to meet and confer to arrange for a third party forensic inspection with agreed upon search terms. The production shall take place within 20 days of notice of this order.
The motion is granted as to Nos. 38 and 43 as the objections are overruled and ordered stricken except for privilege. Any documents withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege shall be identified with particularity.
The motion is denied as to No. 39 as it is overly broad and invades right to privacy without good cause.
The motion is denied as to No. 40 as irrelevant.
The motion is denied as to No. 41 as it is overly broad and invades right to privacy without good cause.
Further responses and production shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. All requests for sanctions are denied. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Quash Or Modify Subpoena For Deposition And Production Of Records, Or In The Alternative, A Protective Order Prohibiting The Obtaining Of Depositoon And Documents Through Subpoena
Off Calendar Jul-08-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted.

PLAINTIFF JAYANT AGARWAL's Motion To Quash Or Modify Subpoena For Deposition And Production Of Records, or, in the alternative, A Protective Order Prohibiting The Obtaining Of Deposition And Documents Through Subpoena is denied. All requests for sanctions are denied. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.
